grant_title: 2025 Bridgeport Environmental Task Force Request for Proposals
eligible_applicant_type: departments of the City’s government referenced in the CEBA
geographic_restrictions: Projects must primarily benefit the neighborhoods surrounding Bridgeport Harbor Station (South End, West End, West Side, Black Rock, East Side, East End) or anywhere in Bridgeport. The ETF interprets the CEBA as allowing support for worthy projects anywhere in Bridgeport.
project_area: Environmental justice, public health, air quality improvement, infrastructure, health services, outreach, education, energy efficiency, and community well-being in Bridgeport.
demographic_requirements: Proposals must advance the intents of the US Environmental Justice Act, focusing on achieving maximum public health and environmental benefit for underserved and vulnerable populations in Bridgeport, particularly in neighborhoods surrounding Bridgeport Harbor Station.
eligibility_summary: Open to individuals, organizations serving Bridgeport, and City government departments referenced in the CEBA. Applicants must propose new project outcomes and accomplishments. Formal applicants do not need to be 501(c)(3) but should consider tax implications. Partnerships are encouraged. Proposals must address long-term public benefit and environmental justice, with favorable consideration given to those leveraging additional non-local funding sources.
